curator: Claude (launch demo set — see CURATION.md; reals sourced + dated, verify before public seeding)

quotes.text: Current observations hint that our standard model of the universe is incorrect.
quotes.speaker: Julie McEnery
quotes.context: Roman Space Telescope senior project scientist, NASA

source.title: Scientific American — NASA's Roman Space Telescope will launch in September

quotes.text: Humanity is about to be handed almost unimaginable power, and it is deeply unclear whether our social, political, and technological systems possess the maturity to wield it.
quotes.speaker: Dario Amodei
quotes.context: Anthropic CEO, on the arrival of beyond-human AI

source.title: TechRadar — Quote of the day: Dario Amodei on AI

quotes.text: We received the first full-colour image from the telescope and the whole control room went quiet. Then someone in the back said, 'that's a lot of galaxies,' and that's now the file name.
quotes.speaker: a Roman telescope engineer
quotes.fake_note: Fake. A charming, totally invented anecdote. Roman hasn't launched yet (targeting September 2026), so there is no first image to go quiet over.

quotes.text: We trained a model only on instruction manuals for six months to see what would happen. It became extremely polite and refused to do anything fun. We shut it down out of respect.
quotes.speaker: Demis Hassabis
quotes.context: supposedly on an AI research podcast
quotes.fake_note: Fake. The deadpan 'we shut it down out of respect' is the tell — but lab-anecdote cadence is real, which is why it fools people.

quotes.text: I wrote four songs this year I refuse to put out, because an AI guessed where the chord progression was going before I'd finished writing it. If a machine can see where I'm headed, I'm headed somewhere else.
quotes.speaker: SZA
quotes.context: supposedly in a magazine cover interview
quotes.fake_note: Fake — but barely. SZA really did talk this year about feeling 'at war because of AI,' so a fabricated quote in that exact spirit is brutal to catch.

quotes.text: People ask if I still get nervous before a show. I'm 85. The only thing I get nervous about is whether the drum stool is at the right height.
quotes.speaker: Ringo Starr
quotes.fake_note: Fake. Ringo did just put out a new record at 85, so the premise is real — the cozy drum-stool punchline is the invented part.
